meta.title: Nobody bills for chasing a 1099 | Coconut for accounting firms
meta.description: For CPA, tax and bookkeeping firms with 3 to 30 staff: a dedicated firm admin who chases documents, runs intake and the portal, and books the calls. Vetted, senior, matched in days.
meta.ogDescription: A dedicated firm admin who chases documents, runs intake and the portal, and books the calls. Vetted, senior, matched in days.

hero.eyebrow: For CPA, tax & bookkeeping firms with 3 to 30 staff
hero.lead: The AICPA puts 17% of an accountant's time in non-billable work. Firms lose 9.3 hours a week to client communication, and another 5 fixing client data. That is a full salary you pay and never bill. Get a dedicated firm admin who chases the documents, runs the portal, and keeps intake moving. Vetted, senior, live in days.

form.step2Sub: We match you with someone who has run client intake and document chasing inside a US accounting firm.
form.needLabel: What's eating your firm's non-billable hours?
form.successText: Keep your phone handy, we'll text you today with the firm admin profiles we matched to your practice.

hours.label: How much help do you need?

wyg.title: A firm admin who has worked inside an accounting practice
wyg.text: Not a generalist pulled off a job board. Someone who already knows PBC lists, portal uploads, e-sign chasing, engagement letters, and what happens to a firm the week before a deadline.

wyg.title: Vetted profiles this week. You pick who to meet.
wyg.text: No cattle call resumes. No agency warm bodies. A real slate of pre-vetted candidates, and you choose who to interview.

wyg.title: Co led interview with your Client Success Manager
wyg.text: Your CSM runs it with you. We know what to listen for when hiring senior operators.

wyg.title: 40 hours of real work, free. Before you commit.
wyg.text: Not a test project. Not a demo. Real chasing, real intake, real inbox, on us, for the first 40 hours.

wyg.title: They run the chase. They never move money or file a return.
wyg.text: No payment authority. No return preparation. You grant the access, at the permission level you choose, and you can pull it anytime.

wyg.title: Managed Talent (month to month) or Direct Hire (one time flat fee)
wyg.text: Your call. Switch models anytime. No long term contracts, no hidden percentages.

wyg.title: Not a fit? We re match, no hassle.
wyg.text: You walk away clean, or we find the right person. Either way, you're not stuck.

ctaBanner.heading: Get your billable hours back.
ctaBanner.sub: Partners back on advisory. Documents still show up. See who fits, then decide.

faq.q: What does a firm admin actually do all day?
faq.a: The work nobody at your firm wants and no client will pay for. Chasing missing documents against the PBC list, running new client intake and onboarding, managing the portal and e-sign reminders, scheduling, triaging the shared inbox, prepping engagement letters, and following up on billing. They work an overlap shift on your business hours and report to you.

faq.q: Do they prepare returns or touch tax data?
faq.a: They do not prepare returns. Whether handling inbound client documents counts as disclosure of tax return information under IRC 7216 depends on your workflow and your permission structure, and that is a call for you and your counsel, not for us. Many firms scope this seat to scheduling, engagement letters, status chasing, and billing follow-up, and keep document intake inside their own portal permissions. You grant the access. You can pull it anytime.

faq.q: Can they move money or touch a client's bank account?
faq.a: No. Intake, chasing, scheduling, and billing follow-up do not require payment authority. Transfers, payments, and filings stay with you unless you specifically choose to set that up. You keep control.

Your admin works inside the systems you grant access to, at the permission level you set, under NDA, and can be removed anytime. Every operator is background checked and vetted before you meet them, and the engagement is managed by Coconut with a named Client Success Manager, not left to a solo freelancer.

faq.q: How much does Managed Talent cost?
faq.a: Plans start at $900 per month. Pricing depends on the role, experience level, and hours. Each plan includes management, payroll, benefits, and support.

faq.q: How does the 40 hour free trial work?
faq.a: Once you pick who to meet and interview them, your matched admin does 40 hours of real work for you, on us. Not a test project or a demo. If it clicks, you keep them. If it does not, you walk away clean.

They notice that someone follows up. Your admin works under your firm's name, from your portal and your templates, on your business hours. Most firms introduce them the same way they would introduce any new coordinator.
Tell us your practice management, portal, and e-sign stack on the call (Karbon, Canopy, Liscio, SafeSend, DocuSign, QuickBooks, Xero) and we match for it before you ever see a profile.

faq.q: How is this different from Upwork or a general VA agency?
faq.a: Those are databases. You filter by price, pick from whoever applied, and carry the risk yourself. We headhunt and vet for your specific role, you interview and choose, and a named Client Success Manager runs the engagement. Our placements hold a 98% retention rate, and if a fit is not right we re-match you at no cost.

faq.q: Will I have to manage them?
faq.a: No. A dedicated Client Success Manager runs onboarding, monitors performance, and handles the day to day. You delegate the work and review the output. You are not building an offshore HR function.

faq.q: How long does it take to get matched?
faq.a: You'll receive your shortlist within 5 to 10 days. Once you've chosen a candidate and completed interviews, you can get started right away. Hire in summer and the seat is trained through the September and October extension deadlines and at full speed on January 2.

faq.q: Can I change or replace my talent later on?
faq.a: Yes. If your needs change or performance dips, we'll replace your talent at no additional cost.

faq.q: What's included in your management and payroll service?
faq.a: We handle contracts, payments, compliance, productivity tracking, and full suite benefits. You get one monthly invoice for everything.

faq.q: Can I switch between part time and full time hours?
faq.a: Yes. You can adjust your talent's schedule as your needs evolve, with a minimum of 20 hours per week to maintain stability for the talent.

faq.q: Can I buy out my talent?
faq.a: Yes. Buyouts are allowed as long as the talent agrees. The buyout fee starts at $3,500 with flexibility based on hours, tenure, and subscription tier.
In the Philippines. Senior, college-educated, English-fluent professionals who work an overlap shift on US business hours and are vetted before you ever meet them.
No long term contracts. Managed Talent is a month to month subscription designed for flexibility and scale.
